title: Wat Tambor and the Quest for the Sacred Eye of the Albino Cyclops
content:
{{Top|fa|rwm|imp}}
{{ComicStory
|image=[[File:WatTamborAndTheQuestForTheSacredEyeOftTheAlbinoCyclops.jpg]]
|title="Wat Tambor and the Quest for the Sacred Eye of the Albino Cyclops"
|writer=[[Michael Murnane]]<ref name="Wat Tambor">{{StoryCite|book=Star Wars: Visionaries|story=Wat Tambor and the Quest for the Sacred Eye of the Albino Cyclops}}</ref>
|penciller=Michael Murnane<ref name="Legends Epic Collection">''[[Star Wars Legends Epic Collection: The Clone Wars Vol. 3]]''</ref>
|inker=Michael Murnane<ref name="Legends Epic Collection" />

|release date=[[April 2]], [[2005]]<ref name="Inside Star Wars: Visionaries">{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/comics/news20050315.html|text=Inside ''Star Wars: Visionaries''}}</ref>
|published in=''[[Star Wars: Visionaries]]''<ref name="Wat Tambor" />
|reprinted in=*''[[Star Wars Omnibus: Wild Space Volume 2]]''<ref name="Omnibus">''[[Star Wars Omnibus: Wild Space Volume 2]]''</ref>

|timeline=c. [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]<ref name="Timeline">"Wat Tambor and the Quest for the Sacred Eye of the Albino Cyclops" ends with [[Wat Tambor/Legends|Wat Tambor's]] departure for the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Mustafar/Legends|Mustafar]], where he is [[Death/Legends|killed]] as depicted in {{Film|III}}. ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' places the events of that film in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]; therefore, "Wat Tambor and the Quest for the Sacred Eye of the Albino Cyclops" must take place in approximately that [[Standard year/Legends|year]].</ref>
|series=
|preceded by=*"[[The Artist of Naboo]]" {{C|''Star Wars: Visionaries''}}<ref name="Visionaries">''[[Star Wars: Visionaries]]''</ref>
*"[[Dark Journey (comic story)|Dark Journey]]" {{C|Legends Epic Collection}}<ref name="Legends Epic Collection" />
|followed by="[[Sithisis]]"<ref name="Visionaries" />
}}
{{Quote|A heart in the eye is where compassion may lie.|The Lord Being of the Swirblies|<ref name="Visionaries" />}}
"'''Wat Tambor and the Quest for the Sacred Eye of the Albino Cyclops'''" is a thirteen-page [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] story written and illustrated by [[Michael Murnane]]. Murnane had previously worked as a concept artist on the [[2002]] [[prequel trilogy]] film, [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']], where he developed the design for [[Wat Tambor/Legends|Wat Tambor]], the story's protagonist. Set within the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity, the comic was published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[April 2]], [[2005]] in the graphic novel anthology ''[[Star Wars: Visionaries]]'', which was created to highlight the work of the artists involved in the film [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']], which premiered on [[May 19]] of the same year.

The narrative follows Tambor as he is summoned by the [[Skakoan/Legends|Skakoan]] [[Elders (Skakoan)|Elders]] to enter the mythical [[Threshold of the Hidden Realms]] to encounter the [[Eye of the Albino Cyclops]]. After receiving a replacement for his right [[Eye/Legends|eye]], which was previously removed by the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], Tambor encounters figures such as the [[Lord Being of the Swirblies]] and the [[Four See Seers of the Cyclops]] before opening a box containing the Eye of the [[Albino Cyclops]]. It reveals a vision of Skywalker to him, and the story ends with Tambor's departure for the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Mustafar/Legends|Mustafar]], where the character meets his [[Death/Legends|death]] at Skywalker's [[Limb/Legends|hands]] in ''Revenge of the Sith''. At the time of its release, the comic was declared to be "possible continuity" by [[Holocron continuity database]] keeper [[Leland Chee]], although concepts originated within it were referenced in other sources.

==Publisher's summary==
''In this offbeat tale, the Techno Union foreman Wat Tambor embarks in a transcendental quest to discover a sacred artifact and to uncover his fate. Remember, "A heart in the eye is where compassion may lie…"''<ref name="Inside Star Wars: Visionaries" />

==Plot summary==
===The quest begins===
{{Quote|The Book of the Boolmide speaks of a singularity. With this grand reveal, the Gates of Grontessiant, Threshold to the Hidden Realms, will show themselves…very soon! Through this threshold lies the Eye! The famed eye of the Albino Cyclops! Your eye—taken by the Jedi Skywalker—may be the key to our success, Tambor.|Elder Clongor, to Wat Tambor|<ref name="Wat Tambor" />}}
[[File:Skakoan_elders.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The Elders meet with Wat Tambor.]]
The [[Skakoan/Legends|Skakoan]]<ref name="Wat Tambor" /> [[Techno Union/Legends|Techno Union]] [[Foreman/Legends|foreman]]<ref name="Inside Star Wars: Visionaries" /> [[Wat Tambor/Legends|Wat Tambor]] of the [[Crimlin clan]] is declared by the Skakoan<ref name="Wat Tambor" /> ruling<ref name="Power Mounds">{{CSWECite|III|41|Power Mounds of the Elders}}</ref> [[Elders (Skakoan)|Elders]] to have cleared the [[Clouds of Cormelish]], necessitating for them to enter<ref name="Wat Tambor" /> his<ref name="Insider 82">{{InsiderCite|82|Drawn by the Force}}</ref> [[mind lattice]]. The Elders summon Tambor, whose right [[Eye/Legends|eye]] is covered by an [[Eye patch/Legends|eye patch]], to the [[Power Mounds of the Elders]],<ref name="Wat Tambor" /> a sacred site on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Skako/Legends|Skako]].<ref name="Planet Hoppers">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/PHSkako|series=Planet Hoppers|text=Skako}}</ref> The Elders describe Tambor as "worthy of the mounds" and a valued yet risky [[Emissary/Legends|emissary]], potentially required by<ref name="Wat Tambor" /> their holy [[Book/Legends|text]],<ref name="Planet Hoppers" /> the [[Book of the Boolmide]]. Tambor accepts their summons and relinquishes his mind to them. The Elder [[Clongor]] states to the foreman that the Book speaks of a [[Singularity (physics)|singularity]] and that the [[Gates of Grontessiant]], which lead to<ref name="Wat Tambor" /> the mythical<ref name="Planet Hoppers" /> [[Threshold of the Hidden Realms]], will open themselves. Clongor also states that past this threshold lies the famous [[Eye of the Albino Cyclops]] and that Tambor's eye, taken by the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], may be the key to the success of their mission.<ref name="Wat Tambor" />

Clongor reads a passage from the Book of the Boolmide: "Pass within… the eye is seen, darkness wields you from unclean." Tambor asks about the supposed entity that exists beyond the gates. Clongor responds that the quest will be a new experience for Tambor, that the Book's symbols are for the Elders only, and that Tambor's desire for revenge for his own eye should not overcome the quest for the Sacred Eye. The Elder also instructs Tambor not to let the Eye's stories blind him. Tambor attempts to excuse himself, claiming that his mind lattice is calibrated for the quickening, but Clongor insists that the foreman must go. One of the Elders gives Tambor a blessing from the Book of the Boolmide, provides a key to the gates and a sacred symbol, and warns him to hurry, as Skywalker will be aware of the mission.<ref name="Wat Tambor" />

===In the Hidden Realms===
{{Quote|Welcome stranger, to your place of danger.|The Swirblies, to Wat Tambor|<ref name="Wat Tambor" />}}
Tambor travels to the Gates aboard a [[Hardcell-class interstellar transport/Legends|''Hardcell''-class interstellar transport]]. He inserts the key and enters a realm of [[Color/Legends|green]] tentacles filled with the [[Swirblies]], small black beings who speak in rhyme. The tentacles begin stripping away Tambor's [[Pressure suit/Legends|pressure suit]] to cleanse him, while the Swirblies state that "A [[Heart/Legends|heart]] in your eye is where compassion might lie" and express knowledge of Tambor's missing eye and desire for a replacement. The symbol given to Tambor begins to levitate, summoning the [[Four See Seers of the Cyclops]],<ref name="Wat Tambor" /> legendary beings who judge whether individuals are worthy of approaching the [[Albino Cyclops]]. Their master,<ref name="Seers">{{CSWECite|I|41|Four See Seers of the Cyclops}}</ref> the [[Lord Being of the Swirblies]], speaks for the Four See Seers, stating that they have seen inside Tambor and that he must purge the impure to receive what he seeks in [[Sleep/Legends|sleep]]. Tambor falls asleep and is observed by the Seers; when the Skakoan wakes, his missing eye has been restored.<ref name="Wat Tambor" />

[[File:Wat_Tambor_enlightenment.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Wat Tambor finds the Eye of the Albino Cyclops.]]
The giant Albino Cyclops appears, along with a small box adorned with an eye symbol. Tambor opens it, revealing the Eye of the Albino Cyclops, and a dark essence flows into Tambor's right eye. A golden light in the shape of a giant eye then emanates from Tambor's eye; the cyclops and Swirblies look on, and the Lord Being declares that "A heart in the eye is where compassion may lie." Looking back into the Eye in the box, Tambor sees a vision of Skywalker's face in it. The Elders declare that Tambor must travel to the fiery planet [[Mustafar/Legends|Mustafar]] to meet his [[Mission to Mustafar/Legends|fate]]. As Tambor arrives on Mustafar with two of the Swirblies on his back, the [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] states that the reader can discover Tambor's destiny by watching [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'']].<ref name="Wat Tambor" />

==Development==
{{Quote|Insipred by a gnarly paintball injury to Mike's right eye, the story's phantasmagoric visuals, idiosyncratic narrative, and mythic underpinnings interact with a personal, perhaps traumatizing, event to produce one of the freshest ''Star Wars'' stories out there.|Frank Parisi, on "Wat Tambor and the Quest for the Sacred Eye of the Albino Cyclops"|<ref name="Insider 90">{{InsiderCite|90|A Message from the Editor|Letter from the Editor}}</ref>}}
[[File:MichaelMurnane.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Michael Murnane, the artist behind "Wat Tambor and the Quest for the Sacred Eye of the Albino Cyclops"]]
"Wat Tambor and the Quest for the Sacred Eye of the Albino Cyclops" is a thirteen-page comic story written,<ref name="Wat Tambor" /> pencilled, inked, lettered, and colored by [[Michael Murnane]],<ref name="Legends Epic Collection" /> a concept artist who developed the design of Wat Tambor for<ref name="Tambor databank">{{DB|character|wattambor|Tambor, Wat}}</ref> the [[2002]] [[prequel trilogy]] film, [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']].<ref name="Year By Year">''[[Star Wars Year By Year: A Visual History, New Edition]]''</ref> The comic was created for the graphic novel anthology ''[[Star Wars: Visionaries]]'', which was initiated by [[Lucasfilm]] senior editor [[Jonathan W. Rinzler|J.W. Rinzler]] as a way for the artists who worked on the [[2005]] prequel trilogy film, ''Revenge of the Sith'', to share their ideas and works that did not appear in the finished film.<ref name="Insider 82" /> The artists had creative freedom in choosing aspects of the ''[[Star Wars]]'' universe to explore.<ref name="Comics Companion">''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]''</ref> Murnane took inspiration from a [[Wikipedia:Paintball|paintball]] injury<ref name="Insider 90" /> he<ref name="Clay Characters">{{SWArchive|url=episode-ii/bts/profile/f20001016/index.html|text=Clay Characters: An Interview with Mike Murnane}}</ref> suffered to his right eye.<ref name="Insider 90" /> ''Visionaries'' was announced on [[StarWars.com]] on [[August 3]], [[2004]], which also revealed Murnane's involvement.<ref name="Visionaries announcement">{{SWArchive|url=eu/lit/comics/news20040803.html|text=Coming Soon: ''Star Wars: Visionaries''}}</ref> The title and a summary of the story were revealed in another StarWars.com post published on [[March 15]], 2005.<ref name="Inside Star Wars: Visionaries" />

==Release and reception==
{{Quote|'Wat Tambor and the Quest for the Sacred Eye of the Albino Cyclops' by Michael Murnane: This story is set before ''Revenge of the Sith'' and follows Techno Union foreman Wat Tambor's surreal journey to find a replacement for an eye he lost by Anakin Skywalker's hand.|A summary of the story published in [[Star Wars Insider 82|''Star Wars Insider'' 82]]|<ref name="Insider 82" />}}
[[File:WTATQFTSEOTAC_concept.jpg|thumb|left|250px|An uncolored page from the comic, included in ''Visionaries'']]
''Visionaries'' was edited by [[Jeremy Barlow]] and published as a trade paperback by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[April 2]], 2005.<ref name="Inside Star Wars: Visionaries" /> "Wat Tambor and the Quest for the Sacred Eye of the Albino Cyclops" occupies third place in the collection, preceded by "[[The Artist of Naboo]]" and followed by "[[Sithisis]]." The comic is also preceded by an uncolored draft image of one of its pages.<ref name="Visionaries" /> 

Barlow felt that ''Visionaries'' as a whole was a success, praising the artists for their variety of stories and expressing that they had justified their positions as employees of<ref name="Insider 82" /> ''Star Wars'' creator<ref name="Year By Year" /> [[George Lucas]].<ref name="Insider 82" /> [[Frank Parisi]], editor of ''[[Star Wars Insider]]'' magazine, described "Wat Tambor and the Quest for the Sacred Eye of the Albino Cyclops" as one of the freshest ''Star Wars'' narratives ever published, praising its unconventional visuals, narrative, and mythical underpinnings.<ref name="Insider 90" /> Artist [[Chris Trevas]], who worked on a variety of ''Star Wars'' projects, described the comic story as "abstract and weird."<ref name="JCF">{{JCFCite|url=posts/25573485|board=Literature|thread=The New Essential Guide to Alien Species|user=Chris_Trevas|date=2006-11-13|time=5:08 PM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20200801193903/https://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-new-essential-guide-to-alien-species.23156129/page-15}}</ref>

''Visionaries'' was later republished by Dark Horse in ''[[Star Wars Omnibus: Wild Space Volume 2]]''<ref name="Omnibus" /> on [[October 9]], [[2013]].<ref name="Dark Horse">{{DarkHorse|url=Books/18-450/Star-Wars-Omnibus-Wild-Space-Volume-2-TPB|text=Star Wars Omnibus: Wild Space Volume 2 TPB}}</ref> "Wat Tambor and the Quest for the Sacred Eye of the Albino Cyclops," along with the stories "[[The Eyes of Revolution]]" and "[[Deep Forest]]" from ''Visionaries'', was also included in ''[[Star Wars Legends Epic Collection: The Clone Wars Vol. 3]]'', published by [[Marvel Comics]]<ref name="Legends Epic Collection" /> on [[April 7]], [[2020]].<ref name="Amazon">{{PenguinComics|url=book/?isbn=9781302923761|text=STAR WARS LEGENDS EPIC COLLECTION: THE CLONE WARS VOL. 3|nolive=1}}</ref> Murnane's story was placed last in the collection, following "[[Dark Journey (comic story)|Dark Journey]]," a comic first published in the [[Star Wars Tales 17|seventeenth issue]] of the comic series ''[[Star Wars Tales]]'' in [[2003]].<ref name="Legends Epic Collection" />

==Continuity==
{{Quote|…the Wat Tambor story is possible continuity as outlandish as it is…|Leland Chee, on the canonical status of the story|<ref name="SWBoards">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=125719&start=0675#10095628|thread=Event Timeline and Character Age Discussion|user=[[Leland Chee|Tasty Taste]]|date=2005-03-29|time=11:02 AM}}</ref>}}
[[File:Anakinwat3tf.jpg|thumb|right|300px|The comic story foreshadows Wat Tambor's death by Anakin Skywalker, portrayed above in a deleted scene from ''Revenge of the Sith''.]]
In response to a fan question, [[Holocron continuity database]] keeper [[Leland Chee]] stated on [[March 29]], 2005 that "Wat Tambor and the Quest for the Sacred Eye of the Albino Cyclops" was regarded as "possible continuity" despite the comic's outlandish presentation and content.<ref name="SWBoards" /> The story's conclusion leads into the events of ''Revenge of the Sith'',<ref name="Wat Tambor" /> which premiered on [[May 19]], 2005,<ref name="Year By Year" /> almost seven weeks following the publication of ''Visionaries'',<ref name="Inside Star Wars: Visionaries" /> in which Tambor is [[Death/Legends|killed]] [[Wikipedia:Offscreen|offscreen]] on Mustafar by Anakin Skywalker.<ref name="ROTS">{{Film|III}}</ref> Tambor's death scene was [[Content cut from Star Wars#Other_cut_scenes_6|filmed but not included]] in the final cut.<ref name="SW cut">{{SW|url=news/the-cinema-behind-star-wars-the-godfather|text=The Cinema Behind ''Star Wars'': ''The Godfather''|nolive=1}}</ref> The ending of the comic suggests that the story takes place shortly before ''Revenge of the Sith'', the events of which transpire in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]].<ref name="Timeline" /> Tambor's eyes are inconsistently depicted as both brown and black in the comic,<ref name="Wat Tambor" /> having been depicted as black in the prequel trilogy films.<ref name="ROTS" /><ref name="AOTC">{{Film|II}}</ref>

Later sources referenced events and concepts that originated in "Wat Tambor and the Quest for the Sacred Eye of the Albino Cyclops." The article "[[Planet Hoppers: Skako]]," written by [[Eric Cagle]] and published on the [[Wizards of the Coast]] website on [[December 9]], [[2008]], explicitly stated that the Power Mounds that appeared in the comic were located on Skako,<ref name="Planet Hoppers" /> [[Retroactive continuity|retroactively]] establishing the comic as the first narrative appearance of the planet.<ref name="Wat Tambor" /> The article also stated that the Gates of Grontessiant opened to a supposedly mythical realm.<ref name="Planet Hoppers" /> ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', also published on December 9 that year,<ref name="Penguin">{{PenguinRandomHouse|url=books/160596/the-complete-star-wars-encyclopedia-by-stephen-j-sansweet-pablo-hidalgo-bob-vitas-daniel-wallace/9780345477637|text=The Complete Star Wars® Encyclopedia}}</ref> included several entries related to the comic, including the Power Mounds,<ref name="Power Mounds" /> the Gates of Grontessiant,<ref name="Gates">{{CSWECite|I|327|Gates of Grontessiant}}</ref> and the Four See Seers of the Cyclops. The latter entry specified that the Seers existed within Skakoan legend and that their role was to look into the hearts and minds of any individuals who passed the Gates to measure their worthiness to approach the Albino Cyclops.<ref name="Seers" /> As part of Expanded Universe media at the time of publication, "Wat Tambor and the Quest for the Sacred Eye of the Albino Cyclops" was categorized as part of the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity in [[2014]].<ref name="Legends announcement">{{SW|url=news/the-legendary-star-wars-expanded-universe-turns-a-new-page|text=The Legendary Star Wars Expanded Universe Turns a New Page}}</ref>

==Legacy==
{{Quote|The Skakoan came from the Visionaries comic. That whole story was very abstract and weird, but I loosely based my Skakoan on the Elder to the left - the one who says 'This one has been chosen to be worthy of the mounds.'|Chris Trevas, explaining an illustration from ''The New Essential Guide to Alien Species''|<ref name="JCF" />}}
[[File:Unknown_skakoan_elder.png|thumb|left|190px|Chris Trevas's image of a Skakoan, inspired by the comic]]
For an illustration of a Skakoan in the [[2006]] sourcebook ''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]'', artist Chris Trevas used [[Unidentified Skakoan (Skako)|one of the Skakoan Elders]] from "Wat Tambor and the Quest for the Sacred Eye of the Albino Cyclops" as a loose inspiration.<ref name="JCF" /> In the current ''Star Wars'' canon, the [[2018]] [[Fantasy Flight Games]] sourcebook [[Fully Operational (sourcebook)|''Fully Operational'']] reintroduced that [[Elders of the Power Mound]]s led the [[Skakoan]] [[religion]] and that one could rise in the ranks of the religion and access significant artifacts. The sourcebook further stated that the Skakoan religion's practitioners believed in an alternate dimension and a mythical creature within it, with only the Elders and a select few others being aware of whether the creature actually existed.<ref name="Fully Operational">[[Fully Operational (sourcebook)|''Fully Operational'']]</ref>
